articles.title: IPL 2026 GT vs MI: Toss Result & Complete Playing XIs
articles.description:
GT vs MI IPL 2026: After four back-to-back losses, Mumbai Indians (MI) are now set to take on Gujarat Titans (GT), who have now won three games in a row, at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad. The two sides feature top Indian and international talent, which promises for an exciting clash in the tournament, which is now entering a decisive phase. The fixture also pits Hardik Pandya, MI's captain, against the side he once led to IPL glory, which makes for an interesting dynamic.

The coin toss for the GT vs MI IPL 2026 match will be held at 7:00 PM IST, with the playing XIs revealed shortly afterwards. Details will be updated here accordingly.

articles.title: Ex-Afghan star battles rare disease at Delhi hospital as family makes emotional appeal: 'Remember him in your prayers'

articles.title: Gujarat Titans vs Mumbai Indians Live Score Updates, IPL 2026: Mumbai Indians seek answers amid tough campaign
articles.description: With just one victory from five matches, Mumbai Indians are under pressure as they prepare to face Gujarat Titans at the Narendra Modi Stadium on Monday. Placed at the bottom of the points table, Mumbai must avoid another defeat, particularly against a side they have not beaten at this venue. The task remains difficult as Gujarat Titans have established a strong record in Ahmedabad. Mumbai’s campaign so far has been inconsistent, especially in bowling. The new-ball duo of Deepak Chahar and Trent Boult has struggled to make early breakthroughs and has conceded runs. Jasprit Bumrah has also not been at his usual best, leaving the side without the control it typically relies on. Shardul Thakur leads Mumbai’s wicket tally with six dismissals, but his economy rate of 13.57 reflects the runs conceded. The spin department has provided some control but has not been particularly threatening. Mitchell Santner, who missed the previous game against Punjab Kings due to illness, has maintained an economical spell, while Afghanistan’s Allah Ghazanfar has taken wickets but has also been expensive. Mumbai’s batting performances have also lacked consistency. Quinton de Kock’s century against Punjab Kings, after stepping in for the injured Rohit Sharma, was a key highlight. However, the team struggled to maintain momentum in the final overs, with the middle and lower order failing to finish strongly, an issue that remains unresolved. Gujarat Titans, on the other hand, have one of the stronger bowling units in the competition. Mohammad Siraj and Kagiso Rabada lead the pace attack with the new ball, while Prasidh Krishna and Ashok Sharma add further pace. Rashid Khan continues to play an important role with his leg spin during the middle overs. Captain Shubman Gill acknowledged after a last-over win against Kolkata Knight Riders that he would have preferred to complete the chase earlier. His remarks highlighted concerns about the middle order, which has depended significantly on Gill, B Sai Sudharsan and Jos Buttler. The match also carries significance with Hardik Pandya returning to the venue where he guided Gujarat Titans to their first IPL title in 2022. While attention around his move back to Mumbai has reduced, his batting form remains under scrutiny. An improvement in his performance could be crucial for Mumbai as they attempt to turn their season around.

The contrast between his intense in-ring persona and this playful gesture added a fresh dimension to his character, giving fans a memorable moment beyond the match itself.

articles.title: Arya–Connolly carnage rewrites IPL history, trail only Kohli–ABD in elite list
articles.description: Punjab Kings' Priyansh Arya and Cooper Connolly etched their names in IPL history with a blistering 182-run partnership off just 80 balls against Lucknow Super Giants. Their explosive display, featuring 16 sixes, now ranks second only to the legendary Kohli-de Villiers stand for scoring rate in IPL history. This remarkable feat highlights Punjab's emerging talent.
